mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production (INDPRO) signals robust manufacturing activity, which correlates with stronger corporate earnings and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods of rising INDPRO are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Below-target inflation (CPIAUCSL < 220) indicates accommodative monetary policy, which lowers real interest rates and drives equity valuations higher over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns are negative in 60%+ of instances where CPIAUCSL remains below 220, or Rank IC < 0.03 in low-inflation regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in FEDFUNDS and CPIAUCSL signal a tightening cycle with inflationary pressures, which raises discount rates and reduces equity valuations over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns are positive in 60%+ of cases where both FEDFUNDS and CPIAUCSL rise, or interaction effect size < 0.05 in regression models.

mechanism_narrative: Strong housing starts (HOUST > 550) reflect elevated consumer demand, which boosts corporate revenues and generates positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day returns are negative in 60%+ of instances where HOUST exceeds 550, or Rank IC < 0.03 in high-housing regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Strong job growth (PAYEMS > 130,000) indicates a tightening labor market, which supports consumer spending and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day returns are negative in 60%+ of periods with PAYEMS above 130,000, or Rank IC < 0.03 in labor-tight regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Declining unemployment (UNRATE < 5%) reflects a strong labor market, which increases household income and generates positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns are negative in 60%+ of instances where UNRATE falls below 5%, or Rank IC < 0.03 in low-unemployment regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in consumer spending (RSAFS > 350,000) and housing demand (HOUST > 550) signals strong aggregate demand, which drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day returns are negative in 60%+ of cases where both RSAFS and HOUST rise, or interaction effect size < 0.05 in regression models.

mechanism_narrative: Weak industrial production (INDPRO < 99) during a monetary tightening cycle indicates supply-side weakness, which reduces inflationary pressures and supports positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day returns are negative in 60%+ of instances where INDPRO < 99 and FEDFUNDS > 1%, or interaction effect size < 0.05 in regression models.
